title: Renal findings in a discharge summary
description: Extract AKI status and CKD stage independently. AKI: use absent when AKI is negated or never explicitly named; present_on_admission only when AKI is documented at admission, on arrival, in the presenting history, or before transfer; hospital_acquired when AKI explicitly develops after admission or during treatment (for example on a hospital day, after contrast, surgery, infection, or diuresis); present_timing_unclear when current-encounter AKI is explicit but timing is unavailable; historical only for remote AKI not active in this encounter. Never infer AKI from creatinine, eGFR, urine output, dialysis, or a nephrology consult. CKD: use not_documented only when CKD is absent or explicitly negated; use documented_stage_unknown when CKD is explicitly documented without a stage; extract 1, 2, 3, 3a, 3b, 4, or 5 only when the note states it; ESRD means stage 5. Evidence must be a short exact quote, not a paraphrase. Evaluate negation, encounter status, and temporal phrases before choosing labels.

aki_status.description: Current encounter AKI timing. A hospital-day or post-treatment onset is hospital_acquired, never present_on_admission. Negated AKI is absent.

ckd_stage.description: Explicit CKD stage. CKD named without a stage is documented_stage_unknown; no CKD or negated CKD is not_documented; ESRD is 5.

evidence.description: A short verbatim quote from the note supporting the selected AKI/CKD labels, or an empty string when neither is documented.
